findings.message: owner_email is added to the ItemRead response schema, which is the external API contract serialized for both GET /items and POST /items. The model definition explicitly marks owner_email as PII that must not be included in external responses (src/demo/db/models.py:16). This echoes the owner's email address to all API clients.
findings.suggestion: Remove owner_email from ItemRead so the email PII is never serialized into external responses. If a client truly needs owner contact, restrict it to an authenticated/authorized endpoint with a documented purpose, or mask the value.

findings.message: ItemCreate collects owner_email from external clients, but create_item only persists name (owner_email is silently dropped). Collecting PII that is not used for the stated purpose violates data-minimization; it sets up an interface that invites future storage/echo of the email.
findings.suggestion: Drop owner_email from ItemCreate unless there is a concrete, documented need to store it. If it is needed, pass it through to create_item intentionally and ensure it is excluded from response/log surfaces.
